mnemonics.title: Embedded Software Types
mnemonics.content: CAF - Coded for Applications (App), Firmware runs first, RTOS handles tasks Real-time

mnemonics.title: ISR Guidelines
mnemonics.content: KISS - Keep ISRs Short and Simple

acronyms.usage: Provides standardized API to access microcontroller peripherals
Manages task scheduling with deterministic timing constraints
Special function executed in response to hardware interrupts
Standard debug interface for in-circuit programming and debugging
Unified tool for writing, compiling, and debugging embedded code
Set of functions for software components to communicate
Non-volatile memory for storing calibration data and parameters

visualTips.visualization: Think of microcontroller memory as a building: Flash is the foundation (permanent), RAM is the workspace (temporary), Stack is the elevator (vertical calls)
Flow like a factory: Source (raw material) → Compiler (加工) → Assembler (parts) → Linker (assembly) → Debugger (QC)
ISR is like an emergency alarm - interrupts normal work, handled quickly, then returns to normal operation
Startup sequence like waking up: Power → Bootloader (alarm clock) → Initialize (stretch) → Load App (start day)

analogies.analogy: Like a translator between two people speaking different languages - converts application requests into hardware commands
Like the BIOS in a computer - the fundamental software that makes hardware work at the most basic level
Like a digital whiteboard - you can write on it, erase, and rewrite, and it remembers what you wrote even when turned off
Like writing a letter in one country to be read in another - you create somewhere but it executes elsewhere
